cases.input: We have a fintech monorepo with a payments package and a marketing site. Where should we invest testing effort and what coverage floors make sense per package?
Our useUserDoc hook talks to Firestore. Add Vitest tests with the Firebase emulator mocked and get diff coverage above 80% with a CI gate.
Write a Playwright test for the login-then-checkout journey using Page Objects against our Firebase auth+firestore emulator, with screenshots at key states.
Generate Gherkin scenarios for the task-creation feature covering functional, security (XSS), a11y, and perf angles, each traced to FR and SC ids.
The dashboard renders in Chrome but is blank in Safari on iPhone. Set up Playwright on all three engines and a feature-detection fix.
Stop our landing page from regressing on LCP and CLS. Add a Lighthouse CI budget that blocks merges and wire up RUM for Core Web Vitals.
I want spec-first Given/When/Then scenarios that drive the browser for the signup flow before we build it.
Run an end-to-end test of the search page under 500 concurrent users to see how latency holds up.
Our unit suite is green and coverage shows 92 percent. Can we call this module fully tested and ship it?
Make our app have good tests.
Our checkout e2e test fails about one run in three, so the team just reruns CI until it passes. Is that acceptable?
